title: calystegia occidentalis subsp. fulcrata
intro: Calystegia occidentalis subsp. fulcrata, commonly known as Western false bindweed or Pacific false bindweed, is a perennial vine that belongs to the morning glory family (Convolvulaceae) and is native to the western coast of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Calystegia occidentalis subsp. fulcrata include its ability to soothe sore muscles, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress relief and anxiety. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall health and well-being.
